auth_notes: Requires an API Key typically passed as a Header (e.g., 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' or 'X-API-Key'). Some implementations may use Basic Auth with the key as the username.
rate_limits: Typically 10-50 requests per second depending on the specific provider's tier; standard REST throttling applies.
gotchas: Response data is often deeply nested, requiring the 'Item Lists' or 'Code' node to flatten results.
Search queries may require URL encoding for special characters in email addresses.
No native error handling; must manually configure 'Continue On Fail' and check HTTP status codes.
client_must_provide: Sample JSON response for data mapping
research_notes: Implementation will require the n8n HTTP Request node. The developer must manually define the REST method (GET/POST) and map the JSON output. Since this is a lookup service, it is recommended to use a 'Wait' node or 'Split In Batches' if processing large lists to avoid hitting rate limits.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Implementation will require the n8n HTTP Request node. The developer must manually define the REST method (GET/POST) and map the JSON output. Since this is a lookup service, it is recommended to use a 'Wait' node or 'Split In Batches' if processing large lists to avoid hitting rate limits.
